figures.caption: Fig. 1: Typical examples of ambiguities in the remote sensing images. We attribute these ambiguities to three main factors: viewpoint, scale, and knowledge.

Fig. 3: Visualization of baseline Change3D [56] and STAND on LEVIR-CC. (a), (b), and (c) are change image pairs, while (d) is a no-change pair.

Fig. 2: The architecture of STAND, including an interpretable transition constraint (ITC), dual-granularity target disambiguation (DGTD) module, and semantic concept anchoring (SCA) module. B and fc denote batch size and fully connected layer.
